content.problemStatement:
The most popular feature on snapchat is Snapchat Streak.

A streak is maintained between two people if **both** of them send **at least one** snap to each other **daily**.

If, on any day, either person forgets to send at least one snap, the streak breaks and the streak count is set to 000.

Chef and Chefina like maintaining their snapchat streak. You observed the snap count of both of them for NNN consecutive days.

On the ithi^{th}ith day, Chef sent AiA_iAi​ snaps to Chefina while Chefina sent BiB_iBi​ snaps to Chef.

Find the **maximum** streak count they achieved in those NNN days.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the number of days you observed.
The second lines contains NNN space-separated integers — A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, the number of snaps Chef sent to Chefina on the ithi^{th}ith day.
The third lines contains NNN space-separated integers — B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​, the number of snaps Chefina sent to Chef on the ithi^{th}ith day.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** streak count they achieved in those NNN days.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains integer N, the number of observed days. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN, the snaps Chef sent each day. The third line contains N space‑separated integers B1 … BN, the snaps Chefina sent each day.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum length of a consecutive streak where on every day of the streak both Chef and Chefina sent at least one snap.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: all three days have both A_i>0 and B_i>0, so longest streak = 3.
Test case 2: Chef never sends a snap, streak never starts, answer 0.
Test case 3: first two days satisfy condition (streak 2), day 3 breaks, later days also break, maximum = 2.
Test case 4: only day 2 satisfies condition, so maximum streak = 1.

solution.approach: For each day we check if Ai > 0 and Bi > 0. This creates a binary array where 1 means the day can be part of a streak. The problem reduces to finding the longest contiguous segment of 1s. Scan the days once, keeping a current length counter that resets to 0 when a day fails the condition, and update the maximum length seen so far. The scan is O(N) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T * N) overall.
